revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kawarazuka Kofun | native_name =瓦塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Kawaraduka Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|29.62|N|139|28|37.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kawarazuka Kofun'''|瓦塚古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kawarazuka Kofun has a total length of 73 meters, and is the sixth largest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Kawarazuka Kofun had a rectangular double moat. A large number of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been[[Archaeological excavation|excavated]] from this tumulus, including ''haniwa'' shaped as houses, waterfowl, men with shields, boys playing musical instruments leading horses, and girls with necklaces, from which a glimpse of the clothes, hairstyles, and festivals of the Kofun period can be deduced. The tumulus dates from the first half of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus has been reconstructed, and is located immediately in front of the {{nihongo|'''[[Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds]]'''|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|Saitama Kenritsu Sakitama Shiseki no Hakubutsukan}} .
;Overall length: 73 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 36.5 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 4.9 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/15/04/12/map/kawara.html Gyoda city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/埼玉古墳群紹介/奥の山古墳 Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds] {{in lang|ja}}
[[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Gyōda]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Sakitama Kofun Cluster]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kawarazuka Kofun | native_name =瓦塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Kawaraduka Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|29.62|N|139|28|37.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kawarazuka Kofun'''|瓦塚古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kawarazuka Kofun has a total length of 73 meters, and is the sixth largest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Kawarazuka Kofun had a rectangular double moat. A large number of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been[[Archaeological excavation|excavated]] from this tumulus, including ''haniwa'' shaped as houses, waterfowl, men with shields, boys playing musical instruments leading horses, and girls with necklaces, from which a glimpse of the clothes, hairstyles, and festivals of the Kofun period can be deduced. The tumulus dates from the first half of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus has been reconstructed, and is located immediately in front of the {{nihongo|'''[[Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds]]'''|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|Saitama Kenritsu Sakitama Shiseki no Hakubutsukan}} .
;Overall length: 73 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 36.5 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 4.9 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline}} *[https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/15/04/12/map/kawara.html Gyoda city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/埼玉古墳群紹介/奥の山古墳 Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds] {{in lang|ja}}
[[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::History of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Gyōda]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Sakitama Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[en:Kawarazuka Kofun]]

{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kawarazuka Kofun | native_name =瓦塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Kawaraduka Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|29.62|N|139|28|37.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kawarazuka Kofun'''|瓦塚古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kawarazuka Kofun has a total length of 73 meters, and is the sixth largest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Kawarazuka Kofun had a rectangular double moat. A large number of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been[[Archaeological excavation|excavated]] from this tumulus, including ''haniwa'' shaped as houses, waterfowl, men with shields, boys playing musical instruments leading horses, and girls with necklaces, from which a glimpse of the clothes, hairstyles, and festivals of the Kofun period can be deduced. The tumulus dates from the first half of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus has been reconstructed, and is located immediately in front of the {{nihongo|'''[[Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds]]'''|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|Saitama Kenritsu Sakitama Shiseki no Hakubutsukan}} .
;Overall length: 73 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 36.5 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 4.9 meters high

The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kawarazuka Kofun has a total length of 73 meters, and is the sixth largest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Kawarazuka Kofun had a rectangular double moat. A large number of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been[[Archaeological excavation|excavated]] from this tumulus, including ''haniwa'' shaped as houses, waterfowl, men with shields, boys playing musical instruments leading horses, and girls with necklaces, from which a glimpse of the clothes, hairstyles, and festivals of the Kofun period can be deduced. The tumulus dates from the first half of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus has been reconstructed, and is located immediately in front of the {{nihongo|'''[[Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds]]'''|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|Saitama Kenritsu Sakitama Shiseki no Hakubutsukan}} .
;Overall length: 73 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 36.5 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 4.9 meters high

The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kawarazuka Kofun has a total length of 73 meters, and is the sixth largest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Kawarazuka Kofun had a rectangular double moat. A large number of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been[[Archaeological excavation|excavated]] from this tumulus, including ''haniwa'' shaped as houses, waterfowl, men with shields, boys playing musical instruments leading horses, and girls with necklaces, from which a glimpse of the clothes, hairstyles, and festivals of the Kofun period can be deduced. The tumulus dates from the first half of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus has been reconstructed, and is located immediately in front of the {{nihongo|'''[[Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds]]'''|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|Saitama Kenritsu Sakitama Shiseki no Hakubutsukan}} .
;Overall length: 73 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 36.5 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 4.9 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kawarazuka Kofun | native_name =瓦塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Kawaraduka Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|29.62|N|139|28|37.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kawarazuka Kofun'''|瓦塚古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kawarazuka Kofun has a total length of 73 meters, and is the sixth largest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Kawarazuka Kofun had a rectangular double moat. A large number of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been[[Archaeological excavation|excavated]] from this tumulus, including ''haniwa'' shaped as houses, waterfowl, men with shields, boys playing musical instruments leading horses, and girls with necklaces, from which a glimpse of the clothes, hairstyles, and festivals of the Kofun period can be deduced. The tumulus dates from the first half of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus has been reconstructed, and is located immediately in front of the {{nihongo|'''[[Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds]]'''|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|Saitama Kenritsu Sakitama Shiseki no Hakubutsukan}} .
;Overall length: 73 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 36.5 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 4.9 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline}} *[https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/15/04/12/map/kawara.html Gyoda city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/埼玉古墳群紹介/奥の山古墳 Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds] {{in lang|ja}}

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kawarazuka Kofun]][[en:Kawarazuka Kofun]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kawarazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kawarazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawarazuka Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawarazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kawarazuka Kofun | native_name =瓦塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Kawaraduka Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|29.62|N|139|28|37.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kawarazuka Kofun'''|瓦塚古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kawarazuka Kofun has a total length of 73 meters, and is the sixth largest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Kawarazuka Kofun had a rectangular double moat. A large number of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been[[Archaeological excavation|excavated]] from this tumulus, including ''haniwa'' shaped as houses, waterfowl, men with shields, boys playing musical instruments leading horses, and girls with necklaces, from which a glimpse of the clothes, hairstyles, and festivals of the Kofun period can be deduced. The tumulus dates from the first half of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus has been reconstructed, and is located immediately in front of the {{nihongo|'''[[Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Burial Mounds]]'''|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|Saitama Kenritsu Sakitama Shiseki no Hakubutsukan}} .
;Overall length: 73 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 36.5 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 4.9 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kawarazuka Kofun | native_name =瓦塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Kawaraduka Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|29.62|N|139|28|37.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kawarazuka Kofun'''|瓦塚古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kawarazuka Kofun has a total length of 73 meters, and is the sixth largest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Kawarazuka Kofun had a rectangular double moat. A large number of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been[[Archaeological excavation|excavated]] from this tumulus, including ''haniwa'' shaped as houses, waterfowl, men with shields, boys playing musical instruments leading horses, and girls with necklaces, from which a glimpse of the clothes, hairstyles, and festivals of the Kofun period can be deduced. The tumulus dates from the first half of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus has been reconstructed, and is located immediately in front of the {{nihongo|'''[[Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns]]'''|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|Saitama Kenritsu Sakitama Shiseki no Hakubutsukan}} .
;Overall length: 73 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 36.5 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 4.9 meters high

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kawarazuka Kofun | native_name =瓦塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Kawaraduka Kofun 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|29.62|N|139|28|37.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kawarazuka Kofun'''|瓦塚古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kawarazuka Kofun has a total length of 73 meters, and is the sixth largest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Kawarazuka Kofun had a rectangular double moat. A large number of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been[[Archaeological excavation|excavated]] from this tumulus, including ''haniwa'' shaped as houses, waterfowl, men with shields, boys playing musical instruments leading horses, and girls with necklaces, from which a glimpse of the clothes, hairstyles, and festivals of the Kofun period can be deduced. The tumulus dates from the first half of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus has been reconstructed, and is located immediately in front of the {{nihongo|'''[[Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns]]'''|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|Saitama Kenritsu Sakitama Shiseki no Hakubutsukan}} .
;Overall length: 73 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 36.5 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 4.9 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kawarazuka Kofun | native_name =瓦塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Kawaraduka Kofun 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|29.62|N|139|28|37.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kawarazuka Kofun'''|瓦塚古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kawarazuka Kofun has a total length of 73 meters, and is the sixth largest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Kawarazuka Kofun had a rectangular double moat. A large number of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been[[Archaeological excavation|excavated]] from this tumulus, including ''haniwa'' shaped as houses, waterfowl, men with shields, boys playing musical instruments leading horses, and girls with necklaces, from which a glimpse of the clothes, hairstyles, and festivals of the Kofun period can be deduced. The tumulus dates from the first half of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus has been reconstructed, and is located immediately in front of the {{nihongo|'''[[Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns]]'''|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|Saitama Kenritsu Sakitama Shiseki no Hakubutsukan}} .
;Overall length: 73 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 36.5 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 4.9 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

The tumulus dates from the first half of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus has been reconstructed, and is located immediately in front of the {{nihongo|'''[[Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns]]'''|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|Saitama Kenritsu Sakitama Shiseki no Hakubutsukan}} .
;Overall length: 73 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 36.5 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 4.9 meters high

It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Kawarazuka Kofun had a rectangular double moat. A large number of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been[[Archaeological excavation|excavated]] from this tumulus, including ''haniwa'' shaped as houses, waterfowl, men with shields, boys playing musical instruments leading horses, and girls with necklaces, from which a glimpse of the clothes, hairstyles, and festivals of the Kofun period can be deduced. The tumulus dates from the first half of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus has been reconstructed, and is located immediately in front of the {{nihongo|'''[[Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns]]'''|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|Saitama Kenritsu Sakitama Shiseki no Hakubutsukan}} .

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kawarazuka Kofun | native_name =瓦塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Gyoda Kawaraduka Kofun 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|7|29.62|N|139|28|37.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gyōda, Saitama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kawarazuka Kofun'''|瓦塚古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kawarazuka Kofun has a total length of 73 meters, and is the sixth largest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Kawarazuka Kofun had a rectangular double moat. A large number of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been[[Archaeological excavation|excavated]] from this tumulus, including ''haniwa'' shaped as houses, waterfowl, men with shields, boys playing musical instruments leading horses, and girls with necklaces, from which a glimpse of the clothes, hairstyles, and festivals of the Kofun period can be deduced. The tumulus dates from the first half of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus has been reconstructed, and is located immediately in front of the {{nihongo|'''[[Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns]]'''|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|Saitama Kenritsu Sakitama Shiseki no Hakubutsukan}} .
;Overall length: 73 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 36.5 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 4.9 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:-inline}} *[https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/15/04/12/map/kawara.html Gyoda city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/埼玉古墳群紹介/奥の山古墳 Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns] {{in lang|ja}}

It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Kawarazuka Kofun had a rectangular double moat. A large number of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been[[Archaeological excavation|excavated]] from this tumulus, including ''haniwa'' shaped as houses, waterfowl, men with shields, boys playing musical instruments leading horses, and girls with necklaces, from which a glimpse of the clothes, hairstyles, and festivals of the Kofun period can be deduced. The tumulus dates from the first half of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus has been reconstructed, and is located immediately in front of the {{nihongo|'''[[Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns]]'''|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|Saitama Kenritsu Sakitama Shiseki no Hakubutsukan}} .
;Overall length: 73 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 36.5 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 4.9 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

native_name_lang = | image = File:Gyoda Kawaraduka Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.124894444444|N|139.47711111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gyoda|WD=Q860659}} | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q2213580}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kawarazuka Kofun'''|瓦塚古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kawarazuka Kofun has a total length of 73 meters, and is the sixth largest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Kawarazuka Kofun had a rectangular double moat. A large number of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been[[Archaeological excavation|excavated]] from this tumulus, including ''haniwa'' shaped as houses, waterfowl, men with shields, boys playing musical instruments leading horses, and girls with necklaces, from which a glimpse of the clothes, hairstyles, and festivals of the Kofun period can be deduced. The tumulus dates from the first half of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus has been reconstructed, and is located immediately in front of the {{nihongo|'''[[Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns]]'''|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|Saitama Kenritsu Sakitama Shiseki no Hakubutsukan}} .
;Overall length: 73 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 36.5 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 4.9 meters high
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Kawarazuka Kofun had a rectangular double moat. A large number of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been[[Archaeological excavation|excavated]] from this tumulus, including ''haniwa'' shaped as houses, waterfowl, men with shields, boys playing musical instruments leading horses, and girls with necklaces, from which a glimpse of the clothes, hairstyles, and festivals of the Kofun period can be deduced. The tumulus dates from the first half of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus has been reconstructed, and is located immediately in front of the {{nihongo|'''[[Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns]]'''|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|Saitama Kenritsu Sakitama Shiseki no Hakubutsukan}} .
;Overall length: 73 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 36.5 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 4.9 meters high
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kawarazuka Kofun]][[en:Kawarazuka Kofun]][[ja:瓦塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kawarazuka Kofun]][[en:Kawarazuka Kofun]][[ja:瓦塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11574186]] Wikidata name: Kawarazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11574186]] Wikidata name: Kawarazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kawarazuka Kofun | native_name =瓦塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Gyoda Kawaraduka Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.124894444444|N|139.47711111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gyoda|WD=Q860659}} | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q2213580}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kawarazuka Kofun'''|瓦塚古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kawarazuka Kofun has a total length of 73 meters, and is the sixth largest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Kawarazuka Kofun had a rectangular double moat. A large number of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been[[Archaeological excavation|excavated]] from this tumulus, including ''haniwa'' shaped as houses, waterfowl, men with shields, boys playing musical instruments leading horses, and girls with necklaces, from which a glimpse of the clothes, hairstyles, and festivals of the Kofun period can be deduced. The tumulus dates from the first half of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus has been reconstructed, and is located immediately in front of the {{nihongo|'''[[Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns]]'''|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|Saitama Kenritsu Sakitama Shiseki no Hakubutsukan}} .
;Overall length: 73 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 36.5 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 4.9 meters high
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kawarazuka Kofun'''|瓦塚古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kawarazuka Kofun has a total length of 73 meters, and is the sixth largest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Kawarazuka Kofun had a rectangular double moat. A large number of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been[[Archaeological excavation|excavated]] from this tumulus, including ''haniwa'' shaped as houses, waterfowl, men with shields, boys playing musical instruments leading horses, and girls with necklaces, from which a glimpse of the clothes, hairstyles, and festivals of the Kofun period can be deduced. The tumulus dates from the first half of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus has been reconstructed, and is located immediately in front of the {{nihongo|'''[[Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns]]'''|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|Saitama Kenritsu Sakitama Shiseki no Hakubutsukan}} .
;Overall length: 73 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 36.5 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 4.9 meters high

It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Kawarazuka Kofun had a rectangular double moat. A large number of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been[[Archaeological excavation|excavated]] from this tumulus, including ''haniwa'' shaped as houses, waterfowl, men with shields, boys playing musical instruments leading horses, and girls with necklaces, from which a glimpse of the clothes, hairstyles, and festivals of the Kofun period can be deduced. The tumulus dates from the first half of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus has been reconstructed, and is located immediately in front of the {{nihongo|'''[[Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns]]'''|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|Saitama Kenritsu Sakitama Shiseki no Hakubutsukan}} .
;Overall length: 73 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 36.5 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 4.9 meters high
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)]]

As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Kawarazuka Kofun had a rectangular double moat. A large number of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been[[Archaeological excavation|excavated]] from this tumulus, including ''haniwa'' shaped as houses, waterfowl, men with shields, boys playing musical instruments leading horses, and girls with necklaces, from which a glimpse of the clothes, hairstyles, and festivals of the Kofun period can be deduced. The tumulus dates from the first half of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus has been reconstructed, and is located immediately in front of the {{nihongo|'''[[Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns]]'''|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|Saitama Kenritsu Sakitama Shiseki no Hakubutsukan}} .
;Overall length: 73 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 36.5 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 4.9 meters high

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kawarazuka Kofun]][[en:Kawarazuka Kofun]][[ja:瓦塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kawarazuka Kofun]][[en:Kawarazuka Kofun]][[ja:瓦塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kawarazuka Kofun]][[en:Kawarazuka Kofun]][[ja:瓦塚古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kawarazuka Kofun | native_name =瓦塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Gyoda Kawaraduka Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.124894444444|N|139.47711111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gyoda|WD=Q860659|1=Gyōda}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q2213580|1=Sakitama Kofun Cluster}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kawarazuka Kofun'''|瓦塚古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kawarazuka Kofun has a total length of 73 meters, and is the sixth largest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Kawarazuka Kofun had a rectangular double moat. A large number of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been[[Archaeological excavation|excavated]] from this tumulus, including ''haniwa'' shaped as houses, waterfowl, men with shields, boys playing musical instruments leading horses, and girls with necklaces, from which a glimpse of the clothes, hairstyles, and festivals of the Kofun period can be deduced. The tumulus dates from the first half of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus has been reconstructed, and is located immediately in front of the {{nihongo|'''[[Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns]]'''|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|Saitama Kenritsu Sakitama Shiseki no Hakubutsukan}} .
;Overall length: 73 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 36.5 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 4.9 meters high
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|WD=Q6573282|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links==

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kawarazuka Kofun | native_name =瓦塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Gyoda Kawaraduka Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.124894444444|N|139.47711111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gyoda|WD=Q860659|1=Gyōda}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q2213580|1=Sakitama Kofun Cluster}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kawarazuka Kofun'''|瓦塚古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kawarazuka Kofun has a total length of 73 meters, and is the sixth largest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Kawarazuka Kofun had a rectangular double moat. A large number of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been[[Archaeological excavation|excavated]] from this tumulus, including ''haniwa'' shaped as houses, waterfowl, men with shields, boys playing musical instruments leading horses, and girls with necklaces, from which a glimpse of the clothes, hairstyles, and festivals of the Kofun period can be deduced. The tumulus dates from the first half of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus has been reconstructed, and is located immediately in front of the {{nihongo|'''[[Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns]]'''|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|Saitama Kenritsu Sakitama Shiseki no Hakubutsukan}} .
;Overall length: 73 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 36.5 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 4.9 meters high
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|WD=Q6573282|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links==

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpōkōenfun]] | name = Kawarazuka Kofun | native_name =瓦塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Gyoda Kawaraduka Tumulus 1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kawarazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.124894444444|N|139.47711111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gyoda|qid=Q860659|1=Gyōda}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|qid=Q2213580|1=Sakitama Kofun Cluster}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kawarazuka Kofun'''|瓦塚古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kawarazuka Kofun has a total length of 73 meters, and is the sixth largest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|1=[[Zenpōkōenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Kawarazuka Kofun had a rectangular double moat. A large number of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been[[Archaeological excavation|excavated]] from this tumulus, including ''haniwa'' shaped as houses, waterfowl, men with shields, boys playing musical instruments leading horses, and girls with necklaces, from which a glimpse of the clothes, hairstyles, and festivals of the Kofun period can be deduced. The tumulus dates from the first half of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus has been reconstructed, and is located immediately in front of the {{nihongo|'''[[Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns]]'''|埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館|Saitama Kenritsu Sakitama Shiseki no Hakubutsukan}} .
;Overall length: 73 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 36.5 meter diameter x 5.1 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide x 4.9 meters high
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|qid=Q6573282|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links==
*[https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/15/04/12/map/kawara.html Gyoda city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/埼玉古墳群紹介/奥の山古墳 Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns] {{in lang|ja}}
